data.title: Maikhan 75MW Pumped Storage Hydro Power Station
data.description: Large-scale renewable energy project featuring a 75MW pumped storage hydroelectric facility serving Mongolia's growing energy needs.
data.content: The Maikhan 75MW Pumped Storage Hydro Power Station is a strategic renewable energy infrastructure project located in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. Our engineering team provided comprehensive services including hydrological assessment, civil design, structural engineering, and environmental impact studies. The pumped storage facility stores energy during off-peak hours and generates electricity during peak demand, providing grid stability and supporting Mongolia's transition to clean energy. The project features advanced turbine technology and modern control systems for efficient operation.

image.alternativeText: Maikhan Pumped Storage Hydro Power Station

outcomes: Energy storage capability for grid stability

data.title: Thames Tideway Tunnel
data.description: Major infrastructure project improving London's sewerage system and protecting the River Thames from pollution.
data.content: The Thames Tideway Tunnel is a 25km 'super sewer' running under the River Thames through central London. As lead design partner, we provided comprehensive engineering services including hydraulic modeling, structural design, and environmental impact assessment. The project will prevent millions of tonnes of sewage from entering the Thames each year, transforming the river's ecology and making it safe for future generations.
outcomes: Preventing 39 million tonnes of sewage overflow annually
challenges: Working beneath one of the world's busiest cities
Minimizing disruption to residents and businesses

data.title: Dubai Metro Red Line Extension
data.description: Expanding Dubai's rapid transit network to serve Expo 2020 and beyond.
data.content: The Dubai Metro Red Line Extension project extends the existing metro line by 15km to connect key areas including Dubai Marina, Jumeirah Beach, and the Expo 2020 site. Our team provided detailed design, project management, and construction supervision services. The extension includes 7 new stations featuring innovative architectural design and advanced passenger information systems.

data.title: Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 5
data.description: Design and planning for the world's most advanced airport terminal.
data.content: Terminal 5 at Singapore Changi Airport will be one of the world's largest and most technologically advanced airport terminals. Our multidisciplinary team is providing master planning, structural engineering, MEP design, and sustainability consulting. The terminal will handle 50 million passengers annually when completed, featuring automated baggage handling, biometric processing, and extensive green spaces.

data.title: Riyadh Metro Network
data.description: One of the world's largest metro projects transforming urban mobility in Saudi Arabia.
data.content: The Riyadh Metro is a comprehensive rapid transit system covering 176km with 85 stations across 6 lines. As design consultant for multiple lines, we provided civil, structural, and systems engineering services. The project includes advanced driverless train technology, elegant station designs by international architects, and integration with bus rapid transit systems.

data.title: Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge
data.description: World's longest sea crossing bridge-tunnel system connecting Hong Kong, Macau and mainland China.
data.content: The 55km Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge is an engineering marvel consisting of bridges, tunnels, and artificial islands. Our team provided specialist advice on marine structures, wind engineering, and construction methodology. The project reduces travel time between Hong Kong and Zhuhai from 4 hours to 30 minutes, promoting economic integration in the Greater Bay Area.
challenges: One of the world's busiest shipping lanes
